findings.message: Temp-file cleanup in _atomic_write is guarded by `except OSError`, so only OSErrors trigger the unlink. A non-OSError BaseException (KeyboardInterrupt during a large write, MemoryError, a signal) bypasses it and orphans the mkstemp temp file '.<name>.XXXXXX.tmp' in the target directory. Not exploitable and not a data-integrity risk — the original file is untouched until os.replace — purely leftover-file hygiene in a local single-user CLI.
findings.remediation: Broaden the cleanup to also cover BaseException (unlink then re-raise), or use try/finally that unlinks tmp_name unless os.replace succeeded, keeping the re-raise so the original exception still propagates.
